rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Deft Ericsson, Inc'S Further Responses To Written Discovery; And To Compel Deposition Of Deft'S Person Most Qualified Custodian Of Records; Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, February 2, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 5. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant Ericsson, Inc.¿s further responses to written discovery, to compel deposition of defendant¿s person most qualified and custodian of records, and request for sanctions is continued to February 16, 2011 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 to provide the parties further opportunity to meet and confer regarding the adequacy of defendant¿s supplemental responses. If any issues remain unresolved, by 4:30 p.m. on February 15 the parties should send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain and then appear at the hearing at 9am on February 16. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Stanley Ho And For The Production Of Documents And Specific Areas Of Questioning; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, February 2, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 5. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the deposition of Stanley Ho and for the production of documents and specific areas of questioning was continued from January 27 to provide the parties an opportunity to submit additional pages from the transcript of Mr. Ho¿s deposition. After review of those further pages, defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc. is given a choice. It must either withdraw the second sentence of paragraph 8 of Mr. Ho¿s deposition or it must produce all documents that Mr. Ho had in mind in his answer on lines 20-22 of page 29 of the deposition transcript and make Mr. Ho available for a further session of his deposition so he may be questioned about the documents. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, February 2, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Flowserve Corporation¿s motion to compel verified responses to discovery will be heard in Dept. 502 at 3:00 p.m., Hon. A. James Robertson II presiding. Defendant¿s motion is granted. Plaintiff¿s discovery responses were due December 10, 2010. Plaintiff¿s proof of service demonstrates they were not served until January 13, 2011 (the court assumes plaintiff meant 2011, not 2010). As plaintiff¿s responses were late, he waived any objections. Plaintiff must serve objection-free responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories, form interrogatories and requests for production within 20 days of the hearing on this motion. Plaintiff must provide defendant with verifications to all of his discovery responses. The requests for admission are deemed admitted, as plaintiff failed to timely respond, and although plaintiff has allegedly served responses since the filing of this motion, they were not sworn and thus are not in substantial compliance with the C.C.P. Sanctions in the amount of $1060 are awarded against plaintiff¿s counsel pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 2033.280(c) and 2023.030. = (220/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KIRAS et al VS. AMCORD, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Verified Responses To The Special Interrogatories Of John K. Bice Co., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, February 2, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant John K. Bice Co., Inc.¿s motion to compel plaintiff to provide verified responses to special interrogatories will be heard in Dept. 502 at 3:00 p.m, Hon. A. James Robertson II presiding. Defendant¿s motion is granted as to plaintiff Sheila Barr. Plaintiffs must provide Ms. Barr¿s verification within 10 days. Defendant¿s request for sanctions against plaintiffs¿ counsel is awarded in the amount of $1065. Defendant¿s motion as to plaintiff Beatrice Kiras is off calendar as moot, as plaintiffs have provided Ms. Kiras¿ verification. = (220/AJR)
